rationale_en: Quality-standards bylaw touches auto and machinery output but regulates specifications rather than volume, so only a modest expected effect on production and value-added shares.
Export-development funds provide real credit and insurance support to exporters but are one of several modest instruments within the much larger non-oil export effort.
Implementing bylaw for rural industrial zones is a genuine channel to small-workshop formation but a narrow administrative lever against provincial workshop counts overall.
Reorganizing peasant holdings into joint-stock farms directly restructured agricultural production units, a real though partial force alongside the White Revolution's other land-reform instruments.
One narrow mechanism within the broader land-reform program has no expected direct channel to national GDP on its own.

The ERAP contract added real incremental exploration and output capacity to the oil sector but was one of several concurrent concession arrangements, not the dominant driver of oil rents or production.
Oil-sector expansion from a single service contract is a marginal contributor to overall GDP relative to the consortium's dominant output.
This concession over an unproven frontier area produced no significant discoveries, so it has no expected effect on oil rents or production.

Concentrating mechanization and investment in designated agricultural poles is a real, targeted channel to farm value added, though one program among several shaping that decade's agricultural output.
This law created the dedicated state company that built Iran's modern cane-sugar agro-industry, a major and fairly direct driver of this narrow commodity's production series.
The annual budget act is the direct legal instrument that sets the very revenue and expenditure totals this chart tracks.
The budget bears directly on government expense but only partially and indirectly on GDP and inflation, netting to a real but partial expected effect across this bundled set of macro charts.
An administrative law governing one bank's internal share-sale proceeds is a narrow, procedural instrument with no expected visible effect on economy-wide credit or listings.
A routine annual budget from the early Reza Shah era is a small fiscal instrument against the whole economy, consistent with the annual-budget-vs-GDP baseline.
Increased Third Plan appropriations directly funded public capital formation, a real but partial force among the many determinants of investment and GDP growth.
A major overhaul unifying corporate tax rates and exemptions is the core statutory instrument defining direct-tax revenue, so a material, near-defining expected effect.
Broad tax reform shapes investment incentives and formal-sector activity, a real macro channel but one force among many behind GDP.
A routine annual budget is a small fiscal force against the whole economy.
A routine annual budget is a small fiscal force against the whole economy.

Exempting production machinery imports from customs duty directly lowers the effective tariff on that machinery subset and eases capital-goods imports, a real but partial channel against the broader tariff-rate and import-growth series.
A mid-year top-up to the wartime budget adds to, but does not define, that year's expenditure and output totals.
The annual budget directly sets government expense and only weakly touches GDP, netting to a real but partial expected effect across this bundle.
Amending specific expenditure and revenue tables adjusts part of that year's budget totals, a real but partial revision rather than the full budget-setting instrument.
Waiving one collateral requirement widens credit access for production and export projects, a real but marginal channel among the many frictions shaping private credit and industrial employment.
A judicial annulment of one bylaw clause is a narrow legal correction, not an economic instrument with expected reach into market capitalization or listings.
A one-year subsidized scrappage scheme is a real but narrow demand channel toward domestic automakers, not large enough to move national vehicle output on its own.

A credit and debt-relief bylaw aimed at preventing factory closures is a real policy response to sanctions-era industrial distress, but its actual expected reach into economy-wide industry and manufacturing value-added shares is modest and execution-dependent.
Industrial support measures for one sector have no expected direct effect on aggregate GDP.

Rules governing investment-bank reserves build real institutional capacity for capital markets, but the effect on market-wide capital-markets indicators is diffuse and administrative rather than a driver of any single series.
As the operational rulebook implementing that year's entire budget execution, this regulation bears directly on the annual budget totals and resource and use breakdown it governs.
A customs schedule confined to imported electronics is a real but narrow channel against the economy-wide tariff mean and total merchandise imports.

As the core rulebook governing how Iran allocates and licenses agricultural water, which consumes over 90 percent of freshwater withdrawals, this bylaw is close to a structural determinant of irrigated-land extent and water-stress measures.
Irrigation allocation rules are a real but indirect input to wheat and cereal yields, which also depend heavily on rainfall, area planted, and prices.
Annual earmarked credit for agricultural inputs is a real but modest financing channel, one of many routine yearly credit bylaws.
Subsidized ICT credit from a telecom profit-share fund is a real but partial channel alongside the much larger private-sector-driven buildout of internet and mobile infrastructure.
Reallocating already-built surplus housing units to welfare institutions does not change the aggregate housing stock the census measures.
Subsidized loan terms for a narrow veteran and martyr-family beneficiary group are too small in scale to expect a visible effect on national housing-unit counts.

A standing regulatory-impact-review requirement is a real structural mechanism aimed directly at the business environment these charts measure, though its effect accrues gradually alongside other determinants of business formation.
Annual earmarked credit for agricultural inputs is a real but modest financing channel, one of many routine yearly credit bylaws.
A narrow bank-credit scheme for university equipment purchases is a small administrative funding mechanism against total national R&D expenditure.
Financing for 18 pilot retrofit projects is negligible in scale against national energy-intensity and per-capita energy-use aggregates.
Technical design standards for social housing units shape unit specifications, not the scale of housing stock the census measures.
Pilot-scale efficiency projects, including a taxi-replacement grant, are negligible against national energy-intensity and consumption aggregates.

Binding investment rules for insurance-company reserves are a real structural regulation of the sector's balance sheets, a material but partial force within the broader financial-sector and insurance category.
The government's insurance-fund contribution is a real, named line item within subsidies and transfers, but one small piece of that much larger aggregate.
Duplicate filing of the same insurance-fund contribution, a real but small line item within the larger subsidies-and-transfers aggregate.

A customs circular that directly amends the applied tariff schedule is a mechanical, near-defining driver of the applied tariff-rate series, with a secondary partial effect on import volumes through price.
Revising specific tariff-code classifications feeds directly into the applied tariff structure, a real but partial revision rather than a full schedule reset.

A court ruling that annuls a standing export and import restriction directly changes the legal permissibility of rice trade, a narrow but central instrument for this specific commodity's trade series.
Voiding one customs-valuation method changes the effective duty base and clearance procedure for affected imports, a real but narrow procedural channel.
Annulling the tax on FX conversion gains directly changes the after-tax return on export proceeds, a real effect on the taxes-on-exports line though only a partial force on total export volumes.

A new treasury-bond mechanism for settling government arrears is a real debt instrument with potentially material scale, though only one channel among several affecting total liabilities in a given year.
The 1972 agreement fixed a substantially higher posted crude price, directly and immediately raising Iran's oil revenue and the weight of oil in exports, an expected structural jump in these series.

As the pricing shock behind the 1970s oil-revenue windfall, this agreement mechanically raised the oil sector's nominal contribution to GDP, a major and fairly direct macro channel for an oil-dependent economy.

Consolidating all foreign-exchange transactions into one state bank established the institutional mechanism that set Iran's official exchange rate for decades, a structural regime-defining law for this series.

Nationalizing water resources gave the state control over allocation and enabled the subsequent dam and irrigation-network expansion, a real but indirect force that worked through later investment programs rather than immediately.
A narrow orchard-land tenure reform within the broader land-reform program has no expected direct channel to national GDP.
Reallocating orchard-land ownership and cultivation incentives is a real but partial channel into fruit and horticultural production specifically, not agriculture broadly.
A routine annual budget is a small fiscal force against the whole economy.
The annual budget law is the primary legal instrument fixing that year's central-government expenditure, revenue, and borrowing, so it directly defines the government-finance totals for that year.
Tax and customs incentives for free trade-industrial zones are a real but modest draw for investment, since these zones account for only a small share of total FDI and capital formation.
A regulatory sandbox confined to free zones is a real but geographically narrow channel into national business-registration and investment-climate indicators.
A legal framework for foreign bank branches is a direct channel in principle, but actual foreign bank presence in Iran has been minimal, so no expected visible effect on the aggregate branch count.
A narrow procedural amendment to an existing collection bylaw has no expected visible effect on aggregate private credit or loan-to-deposit ratios.
The original bylaw sets a structural procedure shaping bank asset quality and lending capacity for years, a real but partial force among the many determinants of private-sector credit.

Amending tariff schedules under the Export and Import Regulations Act is a real trade-policy channel, but a schedule amendment rather than a sweeping reform, so a partial expected effect on trade volumes.
A narrow procedural rule for disposing of seized smuggled goods has no expected effect on legitimate import volumes.
Updating tariff codes on one clearance list feeds into the applied tariff structure, a real but narrow-scope revision.
Exempting a specific list of state-agency imports from duty is a real but narrowly scoped exemption relative to overall customs duties and tariffs.
A single 400-billion-rial bond tranche is negligible against the aggregate stock of government debt and liabilities.
Inter-ministerial coordination of agricultural water demand and supply is a real but diffuse administrative lever on irrigated-land expansion.

Unifying basic health-insurance funds and mandating strategic purchasing is a structural reform of health financing, a real driver of the government expenditure share though one of several forces at work.
Expanding mandatory coverage is explicitly aimed at shrinking the out-of-pocket health-spending share, a real but partial driver alongside other health-financing reforms.
A duty-schedule revision confined to free trade-industrial zones is negligible against total customs and import-duty collection nationwide.
This bylaw defines and operationalizes the guaranteed wheat procurement-price mechanism itself, so it is effectively the series this chart tracks.
The guaranteed price is a floor or reference point, a real but partial input into observed farm-gate wheat and barley prices, which market conditions also shape.
Selling surplus ministry equipment to private farmers is a minor, marginal input-privatization measure with no expected visible effect on the crop production index.
A CNG-station buildout and bi-fuel vehicle mandate is a real, targeted push toward gas-fueled transport, but modest relative to the full national gas consumption, flaring, and injection aggregate.
A one-year production quota for a few tens of thousands of bi-fuel vehicles is negligible against total national vehicle output.

A comprehensive framework covering credit-allocation priorities and sectoral lending ceilings across the entire banking network is a real structural driver of private-sector credit, though one force among ongoing credit-policy actions.
Setting deposit and lending-rate conditions is a direct administrative lever within Iran's regulated interest-rate system, a real but partial driver alongside other rate-setting mechanisms.
A narrow eligibility waiver for one beneficiary group within the Mehr housing program has no expected visible effect on national housing-tenure composition.
A duty-rate adjustment confined to free trade-industrial zones is negligible against total customs and import-duty collection nationwide.

A hiring freeze and cost-cutting directive is a direct annual lever specifically on the government wage bill this chart tracks, though one year's administrative directive among ongoing compensation dynamics.
A recurring minor input-privatization sale mechanism has no expected visible effect on the crop production index.
A real, fairly broad-based agricultural credit-supply mechanism across several subsectors, but an annual routine program rather than a major structural driver of output indices.
This establishes a small, separate free-zone bourse distinct from the Tehran Stock Exchange, with no expected visible effect on the national listed-company or market-capitalization aggregates.
A one-year subsidized renovation-loan program for dilapidated urban housing is small in scale relative to the national housing-unit stock the census measures.
A land-consolidation mechanism directly targeting farm-plot viability is a real structural driver of per-hectare productivity, one force alongside weather, inputs, and seed varieties.
The land-consolidation mechanism's effect on land registration is a secondary, diffuse channel with no expected visible effect on the aggregate agricultural-land series.
This is one implementing bylaw within the broader Article 44 privatization program, a real but partial force reallocating ownership and investment rather than the flagship privatization law itself.

Article 44 divestment via Tehran Stock Exchange listing is a well-documented channel into listed-company and market-capitalization figures, though this specific implementing bylaw is one step among several in the ongoing divestment program.
A continuation of the Article 44 privatization program is a real but partial force on GDP and capital formation, one implementing step within an ongoing multi-year program.

This bylaw continues the mechanism of transferring state-enterprise shares to the Tehran Stock Exchange, a real but partial contribution to listed-company and market-capitalization figures within an ongoing program.
Subsidized loans for a narrow veteran and martyr-family beneficiary group are too small in scale for any expected effect on national housing-unit counts.
A rural housing-improvement subsidy program is real but modest relative to the national housing-unit counts the census measures.
A discovery-reward incentive for private mineral prospecting is a marginal supply-side nudge with no expected visible effect on national mineral rents or provincial mine-production value.
A single state retail chain, however large, is a marginal force against nationwide consumer price inflation.
A targeted loan facility for state agricultural companies is narrow in scope and barely touches private-sector credit at all, with no expected visible effect on either measure.

A newly created market for energy-optimization certificates is a real structural policy tool, but its adoption and impact were likely gradual and limited relative to national energy-intensity aggregates.
